description_long: This object is a rectangular stone ashtray with a rough, weathered surface and a solid, heavy appearance. The top is flat and pitted, likely from years of use and exposure to the elements. Along two opposing sides, there are carved rosette or starburst motifs, adding a decorative touch to the otherwise utilitarian form. The other two sides are plain, with only the natural texture of the stone visible. The edges are irregular and slightly chipped, suggesting age and frequent handling. Its substantial mass and robust construction indicate it is designed for outdoor urban environments, such as parks or public plazas.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone ashtray with a rough, pitted top and decorative carved motifs on two sides. Its weathered surface and solid build suggest it is intended for outdoor urban use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flat, rough top and two prominent carved rosette motifs on the visible side.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the same rough top and a partial view of the carved motifs, with the edge appearing slightly chipped.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain, unadorned stone surface with no decorative elements, emphasizing the object's solid mass.
description_view_3: Opposite front view again highlights the flat top and two carved rosette motifs, mirroring the front.
